Re-read paper sections 5.2-5.3 carefully for missed normalization/cutoff

blockers.blocker: xi_hat zeros from Connes operator do NOT converge to zeta zeros at any N tested (15-120) with L=ln(14). The 'N=30 gives 0.003' claim from Session 21 handoff could not be reproduced.
Root cause unknown: could be L too small, missing paper term, or fundamental misunderstanding of the construction

decisions.decision: gamma_L correction integral has exact closed form: F(e^{L/2})-F(1) where F(u)=-ln(u+1)+ln(u^2+1)/2+atan(u)
decisions.rationale: Substitution u=e^{x/2} gives 2u/((u+1)(u^2+1)). Partial fractions -> elementary antiderivative. Verified to 9e-11.

decisions.decision: beta_L(0) works from general formula with no special case
decisions.rationale: At n=0: coeff = 2L/(-iL) = 2i, complex arithmetic handles it. Sign error in earlier manual limit was the bug.

decisions.decision: OFFSET is genuinely constant — n^2 variation was quadrature error in eq(4.4)
decisions.rationale: Tested at n=0,120 with nq=5000,20000,50000. Variation scales exactly as 1/nq^2. True OFFSET = 1.33690541816.

Tested TAU (mpmath.eig 200dp + numpy), full QW (exact Prop4.3), connes_exact.py (formula 3.14). All give g1_acc >= 0.76. Sweep N=15..60 shows plateau at ~1.1. Original claim may have been from transient session state.

decisions.decision: Eigenvector selection was bugged but it's not the root cause
decisions.rationale: Old code used min(eval) instead of min(|eval|). Fixed, but neither selection gives good xi_hat zeros.

next_action: SESSION 23: Three options. (1) Try larger L — lambda^2=100 or 1000 instead of 14. The paper may need L >> 1 for convergence. (2) Re-read paper sections 5.2-5.3 for delta_N normalization and xi_hat definition — we may be missing a term. (3) Pivot to Rayleigh quotient test which doesn't need xi_hat zeros to converge.
context_notes: 22 scripts. connes_session22.py has exact gamma_L + full QW builder. Key formula: corr = F(e^{L/2})-F(1), F(u)=-ln(u+1)+ln(u^2+1)/2+atan(u). OFFSET=1.33691 (constant). Python: /c/Python313/python.exe, mp.dps=200.
